calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-23-2003 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: Notice having been given and there being no appearance or an opposition filed. The court orders case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court Section 225. Proceedings reported by: Leticia Perez. (212)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-18-2002 Case Settled - Set For Crc 525 Hearing On Oct-21-03 At 10:00 A.M. In Dept 212

MOTION For Order Setting Aside Order Sustaining Demurrers To Answer And Cross-Complaint;
Off Calendar Jan-14-2002 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Lori B. Feldman, Esq. 492-2120 for defendants and moving party
Nicholas G. Soter, Esq. and Jeremy Meltzer, Esq. of 285-8010 for Plaintifs MURSHED ALGAHEIM and ABDUL RAQIB ALGAHEIM opposing party

ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING -- Defendants' motion to set aside the Court's order sustaining demurrers to answer and the cross-complaint is DENIED without prejudice to renewal at such time that Defendant is able to comply with CCP 473(b) (jjm/KH) Eric L. Throne, CSR 7855

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-27-2001 Continued To Mar-25-02 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 212

DEMURRER
Off Calendar Dec-10-2001 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jeffrey S. Bolender, Esq. of MONROE & SHAPIRO, LLP 310/670-1381 for intervenor NAUTILUS INSURANCE COMPANY moving party
Nicholas G. Soter, Esq. and Jeremy Meltzer, Esq. 285-8010 for Plaintiffs MURSHED ALGAHEIM and ABDUL RAQIB ALGAHEIM opposing party

The Court announced its tentative ruling as follows: Denied without prejudice. Defendants have a new attorney. Reliance Insurance Company vs. Superior Court is inapplicable. The demurrer to intervention in complaint is off calendar. Motion for leave to intervene is denied without prejudice.

Counsel having heard the tentative ruling ARGUED THE MATTER AND THE COURT REVERSED ITSELF AND SUSTAINED THE DEMURRER WITH LEAVE TO REASSERT IF NEW COUNSEL IS NOT ADEQUATELY REPRESENTED BY THE INSURED. (ajr/kim) Joseph Vickstein CSR 4780.
